title: What to know about Salvia officinalis before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Salvia officinalis are the relief of digestive issues, such as indigestion, due to the presence of carvacrol, a compound that exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oil of Salvia officinalis has also been found to effectively treat respiratory problems, including cough and respiratory issues, by inhibiting the production of mucus and reducing inflammation. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has been used to alleviate anxiety, fever, and inflammation, likely due to the presence of rosmarinic acid, a compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's antispasmodic properties, attributed to its content of borneol, make it a potential treatment for diarrhea and skin conditions, such as eczema, by soothing the digestive tract and reducing inflammation. Furthermore, Salvia officinalis has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and inflammation, likely due to its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Salvia officinalis are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is largely due to its bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and saponins. Its antioxidant properties, resulting from the presence of volatile oils, help protect cells from oxidative damage and contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects, which are further enhanced by the plant's ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, the analgesic properties of Salvia officinalis, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, play a significant role in pain relief, while its antispasmodic properties, resulting from the action of volatile oils, help alleviate muscle spasms. The plant's antihistamine properties, which are largely due to the presence of flavonoids, help alleviate allergic reactions, and its cardiovascular protector effects, resulting from the action of flavonoids and saponins, help reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. Salvia officinalis's neuroprotective properties, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the brain from oxidative damage and may help alleviate neurodegenerative diseases.
identification: Salvia officinalis is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its upright growth habit, grayish-green leaves with crenate margins, and terminal spikes of blue to purple flowers. Native to the Mediterranean region, this species has been naturalized in many parts of the world, including Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, typically growing in well-drained soils and full sun to partial shade. The plant can grow up to 75 cm in height, with a spread of around 45 cm, and its leaves are often used as an ornamental in gardens and landscapes. Salvia officinalis is found in a variety of habitats, including dry rocky areas, scrublands, and woodland edges, and is often associated with calcareous soils. The plant's growth is adapted to survive dry periods, with a deep taproot allowing it to access water deep in the soil.
cultivation: Salvia officinalis is a hardy perennial herb that exhibits a shrub-like growth habit, typically growing 1-2 feet tall and 1-3 feet wide. It prefers well-drained soil with a pH between 6.0 and 7.0, full sun to partial shade, and moderate watering, requiring around 20-40 inches of annual rainfall. Growth tips include pruning back established plants in late spring to promote bushy growth, dividing and replanting every 3-4 years to maintain vigor, and providing protection from extreme cold and wet conditions. Salvia officinalis is also known to be drought-tolerant and can thrive in areas with low fertility, making it a suitable choice for gardeners with challenging soil conditions. Regular harvesting of the leaves in the summer months can encourage new growth and prevent the plant from flowering, allowing for continuous use throughout the growing season.
history: Salvia officinalis is an herb that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with the ancient Greeks and Romans utilizing it to treat a variety of ailments, including indigestion, melancholy, and sore throats. The herb contains compounds such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In the Middle Ages, Salvia officinalis was used to treat conditions such as epilepsy, gout, and ulcers, often in combination with other herbs. The plant's leaves and flowers were also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to their expectorant properties. Historical records also suggest that Salvia officinalis was used to treat fever and even the plague.

constituents.name: Rosmarinic acid
constituents.description: Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties

constituents.name: Ursolic acid
constituents.description: Ursolic acid is a pentacyclic triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, as well as the ability to inhibit histone deacetylase activity and modulate the immune response.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid that has antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Luteolin
constituents.description: Luteolin is a flavone with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Apigenin
constituents.description: A flavone compound found in Salvia officinalis, with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Oleanolic acid
constituents.description: Oleanolic acid is a pentacyclic triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Thymol
constituents.description: Thymol is a phenolic monoterpene with antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties.

constituents.name: Carnosic acid
constituents.description: A lipophilic antioxidant with potent free radical-scavenging and anti-inflammatory properties, and the ability to inhibit lipid peroxidation and DNA damage.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Salvianolic acid
constituents.description: A water-soluble phenolic acid with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-apoptotic properties.

constituents.name: Caffeic acid
constituents.description: Caffeic acid is a phenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Carvacrol
constituents.description: Carvacrol is a phenolic monoterpene with antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties

constituents.name: Naringenin
constituents.description: Naringenin is a flavonoid found in Salvia officinalis, which exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Caryophyllene
constituents.description: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and anti-bacterial properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins are astringent, antimicrobial, and antiseptic compounds found in Salvia officinalis, contributing to its antifungal and antibacterial properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids from Salvia officinalis are a group of compounds that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Salvia officinalis flavonoids are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a type of bioactive compound found in Salvia officinalis that have hemolytic, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties.

constituents.name: Thujone
constituents.description: Thujone is a bioactive monoterpene ketone constituent of Salvia officinalis, exhibiting neurostimulant and sedative properties.

constituents.name: Thymonin
constituents.description: Thymonin is a sesquiterpene coumarinoid with antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Ingestion of Salvia officinalis can cause stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its saponin and thymol content.
Contact dermatitis, urticaria, and angioedema have been reported in individuals allergic to Salvia officinalis.
Salvia officinalis may cause a significant increase in heart rate and blood pressure, potentially leading to hypertension and cardiac arrhythmias.
Ingestion of excessive Salvia officinalis extracts can cause liver injury due to the presence of thujone, a hepatotoxic compound.
Salvia officinalis contains compounds that affect thyroid function and influence thyroid hormone levels.
Salvia officinalis consumption has been associated with altered perception, confusion, disorientation, dizziness, euphoria, hallucinations, nausea, and tremors.
Long-term Salvia officinalis consumption can lead to kidney damage due to its high thujone content, which is toxic to the kidneys in large doses.
Consuming high doses of Salvia officinalis essential oil or extracts can induce seizure activity in susceptible individuals due to the presence of thujone.

High doses of Salvia officinalis, particularly among individuals with a history of psychiatric disorders, may trigger or exacerbate psychotic episodes due to its potent impact on the central nervous system and serotonin system disruption.
Respiratory failure due to severe respiratory depression caused by salvinorin A, a potent opioid compound in Salvia officinalis.

intro_description: Salvia officinalis, commonly known as common sage, is a perennial herb used for its medicinal and culinary properties. It is traditionally used to aid memory and cognitive function, as well as to treat conditions such as Alzheimer's disease, dementia, and digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome. The herb has several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Its therapeutic properties include antiseptic, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, which contribute to its ability to relieve respiratory issues like bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive compounds present in Salvia officinalis include rosmarinic acid, ursolic acid, and camphor, which can be extracted and used to make tea, tinctures, and essential oils.

uses_list.description: It has been traditionally used to alleviate anxiety and restlessness due to its effects on the nervous system, particularly its ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Its sedative properties have been utilized to treat insomnia and promote a good night's sleep, thereby helping to regulate sleep patterns.
The plant's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties are used to treat diarrhea by reducing inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract and alleviating spasms.
Its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties have been traditionally used to treat migraines, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate muscle spasms associated with the condition.

The plant's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties are used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome, by reducing inflammation and alleviating muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract.
It contains compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various inflammatory conditions, including arthritis and skin irritations.
Some studies suggest that the plant may help to manage blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in treating diabetes.
The plant's antispasmodic properties have been traditionally used to treat asthma by reducing muscle spasms in the bronchial tubes and alleviating respiratory distress.
Some studies suggest that the plant may help to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy in treating hypertension.
Its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties are used to treat gastritis, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate muscle spasms in the stomach and duodenum.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to treat various health conditions, primarily anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties. Additionally, it is used to alleviate digestive issues such as diarrhea and gastroenteritis by reducing gut inflammation and improving gut health. Furthermore, Salvia officinalis is used to manage hypertension, diabetes, and respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma, as well as to relieve coughs and migraines, and reduce menstrual cramps. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also aid in wound healing, and have been studied for their potential to reduce stress and memory loss. Moreover, Salvia officinalis is used to treat gastroesophageal reflux, epilepsy, and to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to treat inflammation by reducing oxidative stress and promoting healing. It is also used to alleviate diarrhea due to its antispasmodic properties, which help relax the smooth muscles in the intestinal tract. Additionally, the plant is employed to treat indigestion by enhancing digestion and relieving spasms in the digestive system. Furthermore, Salvia officinalis is used to calm anxiety and alleviate digestive issues by regulating gut motility and secretion. Its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties also make it effective in treating coughs, migraines, diabetes, dyspepsia, insomnia, hypertension, fever, bronchitis, rheumatism, gastroenteritis, epilepsy, vomiting, headaches, and stomach pain.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to treat anxiety through its adaptogenic properties, which help balance the body's response to stress. It also alleviates digestive issues by regulating gut motility and enhancing the secretion of digestive enzymes. Additionally, Salvia officinalis is used to address insomnia by reducing inflammation in the brain and promoting relaxation. Furthermore, it has been found to alleviate migraines by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, and its anti-diarrheal properties help in managing diarrhea. Salvia officinalis is also effective in managing diabetes by reducing blood sugar levels, and its anti-asthmatic properties alleviate asthma symptoms by relaxing airway muscles, while also providing relief from gastritis, migraines, stress, wound healing, inflammation, epilepsy, hypertension, arthritis, bronchitis, memory loss, skin infections, dyspepsia, and gastrointestinal issues.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea, which is alleviated by its antimicrobial properties. The essential oil of Salvia officinalis is also used to soothe gastritis, while its leaves are known to promote relaxation and reduce insomnia. Additionally, it is used to treat asthma due to its bronchodilatory effects, and to alleviate the pain associated with rheumatism. Furthermore, its anxiolytic properties make it a treatment for anxiety, and it has been found to help manage hypertension by reducing blood pressure. Salvia officinalis is also used to treat indigestion, nausea, menstrual cramps, cough, migraine, hiccups, fever, dyspepsia, digestive issues, bronchitis, flatulence, diabetes, and arthritis.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Salvia officinalis is used to alleviate migraines, a debilitating condition caused by vascular issues, through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's anxiolytic effects also make it a treatment for anxiety disorders, while its sedative properties can help patients with insomnia. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms in diabetes patients. Its carminative properties can also treat nausea and indigestion, making it a natural remedy for stress and gastritis. Furthermore, the plant's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects can be used to treat various conditions including arthritis, digestive issues, hypertension, diarrhea, cough, asthma, epilepsy, gastroenteritis, menstrual cramps, hiccups, and respiratory issues.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the body. These compounds, including triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

The plant's essential oils, particularly carvacrol and thymol, have antimicrobial properties that help to suppress the growth of certain bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms. These compounds have been shown to disrupt the cell membranes of microorganisms, ultimately leading to their death and preventing the spread of infection.

The plant's leaves contain compounds that have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the digestive system, helping to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), bloating, and cramps. These compounds, including volatile oils and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, thereby easing digestive issues.

The plant's extracts have been shown to have a hypoglycemic effect, helping to lower blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes. The compounds responsible for this effect, including carnosic acid and rosmanol, have been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce glucose production in the liver.

The plant's essential oils, particularly carvacrol and borneol, have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the uterus, helping to alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and relax the uterine muscles, thereby providing relief from menstrual cramps.

The plant's extracts have been shown to have a neuroprotective effect, helping to improve cognitive function and memory. The compounds responsible for this effect, including carnosic acid and ursolic acid, have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, thereby promoting healthy brain function.

The plant's essential oils, particularly linalool and borneol, have been shown to have an anxiolytic effect, helping to reduce anxiety symptoms and promote relaxation. These compounds help to reduce stress and anxiety by promoting the release of neurotransmitters such as GABA and serotonin.

The plant's extracts have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the immune system and prevent illness. The compounds responsible for this effect, including triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, have been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells and activate immune cells, thereby promoting healthy immune function.

The plant's extracts have been shown to have antineoplastic effects, helping to fight cancer cells and inhibit tumor growth. The compounds responsible for this effect, including carnosic acid and ursolic acid, have been shown to induce apoptosis in cancer cells, inhibit angiogenesis, and reduce oxidative stress, thereby preventing the growth and spread of cancer cells.

The plant's extracts have been shown to have a hypotensive effect, helping to regulate blood pressure in individuals with hypertension. The compounds responsible for this effect, including carnosic acid and rosmanol, have been shown to improve endothelial function, reduce inflammation, and promote vasodilation, thereby reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health.

benefits_description: Salvia officinalis reduces inflammation due to its high content of rosmarinic acid, a polyphenol with potent anti-inflammatory properties. Suppressing bacterial growth, it contains carnosic acid, a compound that inhibits the growth of various bacteria, making it effective against infections. Eases digestive issues by relaxing smooth muscle and improving gut motility, while lowering blood sugar levels through its insulin-sensitizing effects. Relieves menstrual cramps by reducing prostaglandin production, improves cognitive function through its antioxidant properties, and reduces anxiety symptoms by modulating the GABA receptor. Boosting immune system, it contains thymol, a compound with immunomodulatory effects, and fights cancer cells through its antioxidant and anti-proliferative activities, also regulating blood pressure through its vasodilatory effects.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Salvia officinalis reduces blood pressure due to its vasodilatory properties, which enable the plant to relax blood vessels and improve circulation flow. This reduction in blood pressure subsequently boosts heart function, allowing for more efficient pumping of blood throughout the body, while also lowering cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of dietary cholesterol in the gut. The plant's antioxidant properties also enhance vascular health by neutralizing free radicals that can damage blood vessels, thereby regulating blood flow and improving oxygen delivery to tissues. Furthermore, Salvia officinalis reduces inflammation markers by exerting anti-inflammatory effects, which supports cardiac health by preventing the development of atherosclerosis, a condition characterized by the buildup of plaque in blood vessels, and prevents blood clots from forming by inhibiting the activity of platelets. By reducing blood pressure, improving circulation flow, and preventing blood clots, Salvia officinalis helps maintain a healthy circulatory system.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Salvia officinalis reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which also soothe digestive issues and prevent bloating by suppressing the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut. The plant's antimicrobial and antioxidant properties relieve constipation by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, while also improving absorption of essential nutrients. Salvia officinalis calms irritation in the digestive tract and heals ulcers through its anti-ulcerogenic properties, protecting the gut from oxidative stress and inflammation. This protection also eases diarrhea by regulating the balance of gut bacteria and boosts motility by stimulating the contractions of the intestinal muscles.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Salvia officinalis regulates hormonal balance by exerting its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties, which also lower cortisol levels by reducing the production of stress hormones and subsequently supporting thyroid function, while its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties increase insulin sensitivity by enhancing the uptake of glucose in cells. The plant's ability to stabilize adrenal function is attributed to its adaptogenic and anti-stress properties, which in turn reduce inflammation response by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Salvia officinalis also improves pancreatic health by modulating the secretion of digestive enzymes and enhancing the function of pancreatic beta cells, thereby protecting pituitary gland and regulating prolactin levels. Additionally, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties enhance ovarian function by promoting the production of reproductive hormones and regulating ovulation, and its antimicrobial properties prevent infections that can compromise pancreatic health. By exerting its adaptogenic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, Salvia officinalis also improves pancreatic health and regulates hormonal balance by stabilizing adrenal function.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Salvia officinalis reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to mitigate the symptoms of irritated skin. Accelerating wound healing is facilitated by the antimicrobial properties of Salvia officinalis, which inhibit the growth of pathogens and promote tissue repair. Protecting skin cells from oxidative damage is achieved through the antioxidant properties of Salvia officinalis, which neutralize free radicals and prevent cell degeneration. Fading age spots is a result of the tyrosinase-inhibiting properties of Salvia officinalis, which reduce melanin production and lighten hyperpigmented areas. Salvia officinalis also relaxes tension, soothes irritations, stimulates collagen production, fights fungal infections, preserves skin elasticity, and calms eczema due to its adaptogenic, soothing, collagen-stimulating, antifungal, elastogenic, and anti-inflammatory properties respectively.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Salvia officinalis stimulates lymph flow by its anti-inflammatory properties, which also support lymph node function. This plant enhances lymphatic drainage due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, allowing it to reduce swelling quickly. Additionally, Salvia officinalis improves lymphatic circulation through its vasodilatory action, which also boosts the immune system by increasing the production of white blood cells. The anti-inflammatory compounds in Salvia officinalis also reduce lymph inflammation and soothe lymphatic irritation, while its diuretic properties increase lymphatic filtration, accelerating detoxification. By combining these properties, Salvia officinalis supports the overall health of the lymphatic system.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Salvia officinalis relieves joint pain through its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with arthritis symptoms. The plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties also play a role in treating arthritis symptoms by minimizing oxidative stress and preventing infections. Additionally, Salvia officinalis reduces muscle inflammation, eases muscle spasms, and soothes sore joints, thanks to its ability to modulate the body's inflammatory response and relax tense muscles. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also improve bone density by promoting healthy bone metabolism and reducing oxidative stress. Salvia officinalis also cures muscle cramps, decreases muscle tension, and reduces muscle fatigue, allowing for relaxation and a decrease in muscle fatigue.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Salvia officinalis relaxes nervous system through its adaptogenic properties, which help regulate the body's response to stress. Improved memory function is also attributed to Salvia officinalis due to its antioxidant properties, which protect the brain from oxidative stress and promote healthy neuronal function. Salvia officinalis reduces anxiety attacks by exerting a calming effect on the nervous system, which is largely attributed to its antispasmodic properties that help relax muscle spasms. Enhanced cognitive clarity and increased mental alertness are also benefits of Salvia officinalis, as it contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to improve attention and executive function. By regulating sleep patterns, calming nervous tension, supporting mood stability, reducing stress hormones, and stabilizing nerve function, Salvia officinalis provides a comprehensive range of benefits for the nervous system.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Salvia officinalis regulate hormones by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to stabilize the delicate balance of hormone production in the body. The herb's antimicrobial properties improve fertility by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the reproductive system. Additionally, Salvia officinalis boost libido due to its ability to increase dopamine and serotonin levels, stimulating the nervous system. The herb's estrogenic properties enhance reproductive health by promoting the growth and development of reproductive tissues, while its anti-inflammatory properties stabilize menstrual cycles, alleviating symptoms of PMS. Salvia officinalis treat endometriosis and reduce infertility by inhibiting the growth of endometrial tissue, increasing ovulation, curing polycystic ovary syndrome, and relieving menopausal symptoms.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Salvia officinalis relieves congestion by reducing the viscosity of mucus, making it easier to expel. Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the airways, preventing bronchitis and soothing irritation. Antimicrobial properties of Salvia officinalis combat infections that can cause respiratory problems, while its expectorant properties promote the expulsion of mucus, further relieving congestion. Salvia officinalis also cools down coughs and heals respiratory tissues damaged by infections or irritation. Additionally, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties ease wheezing and reduce the severity of respiratory conditions.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Salvia officinalis prevents bladder stones by inhibiting the formation of oxalate crystals, and it reduces inflammation in the urinary tract due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Salvia officinalis treats urinary infections caused by bacteria such as E. coli, thanks to its antimicrobial properties, which are effective against a wide range of microorganisms. The antioxidant properties of Salvia officinalis also relax muscles in the urinary system, preventing spasms and cramps, and ease discomfort associated with urinary tract issues. Moreover, Salvia officinalis protects kidneys from damage caused by toxins and heavy metals due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, and it cures infections by eradicating pathogens. Additionally, Salvia officinalis soothes irritation in the urinary tract, tightens pelvic muscles, and heals stones by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation.

properties_list.description: Salvia officinalis has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain strains of bacteria, including Escherichia coli and Staphylococcus aureus, due to its essential oils and phenolic compounds.

Salvia officinalis contains antioxidants such as carnosic acid and ursolic acid, which can scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, potentially protecting against cell damage and inflammation.

The anti-inflammatory properties of Salvia officinalis are attributed to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, such as nitric oxide and prostaglandins, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions like arthritis.

Salvia officinalis has been reported to exhibit analgesic properties, likely due to the presence of compounds like caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, which can help to reduce pain by inhibiting the release of pain-related neurotransmitters.

The essential oils and phenolic compounds present in Salvia officinalis have been found to exhibit antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax smooth muscle and alleviate conditions like asthma, bronchitis, and irritable bowel syndrome.

Salvia officinalis has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus and influenza virus, due to its ability to inhibit viral replication and induce apoptosis in infected cells.

The antiseptic properties of Salvia officinalis are attributed to its ability to inhibit the growth of microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of phenolic compounds like carnosic acid and ursolic acid.

Salvia officinalis has been traditionally used as a cicatrizant, likely due to its ability to promote wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue regeneration, and reducing inflammation.

The hepatoprotective properties of Salvia officinalis are attributed to its ability to reduce liver damage and inflammation caused by toxic substances, likely due to the presence of antioxidants like carnosic acid and ursolic acid.

parts_description: The most used parts of Salvia officinalis for medicinal purposes are leaves and flowers. The leaves of Salvia officinalis contain primary bioactive compounds such as rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The flowers of Salvia officinalis are rich in flavonoids, particularly apigenin and luteolin, and volatile oils, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive compounds in both parts contribute to the herb's analgesic, antiseptic, and adaptogenic effects. The combined bioactive compounds in the leaves and flowers of Salvia officinalis make it a valuable medicinal plant for treating various health conditions.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in major bioactive compounds including rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and ursolic acid, which possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, providing primary health benefits such as improved cognitive function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune system function.

parts_list.name: flowers
parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant contain major bioactive compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, conferring primary health benefits such as relief from anxiety and stress, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced immune system function.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is a concentrated liquid used to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and coughs due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as for its expectorant and antiseptic effects.
preparations.preparation_overview: Salvia officinalis tincture is used to treat anxiety, insomnia, digestion issues, cough, diabetes, wounds, and inflammation. This herbal preparation is widely used due to its effectiveness and availability of the plant, making it one of the most common used tinctures. One of the most common effects of Salvia officinalis tincture is its ability to reduce stress and anxiety levels, making it a popular choice for those seeking natural remedies. The preparation is made by steeping the leaves and roots of the Salvia officinalis plant in a solvent, typically ethanol or glycerin, to extract the bioactive compounds. While it's not extremely difficult to make, the process does require some knowledge and attention to detail to achieve the desired consistency and potency.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves and roots from a trusted source.
Clean and chop the Salvia officinalis leaves and roots into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves and roots in a clean glass jar with a lid, leaving about an inch of space at the top.
Pour a neutral solvent, such as 80-proof vodka or glycerin, over the leaves and roots, ensuring they are completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to facilitate extraction.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Essential oil made from this plant is used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to treat wounds, sore throats, coughs and digestive issues.
preparations.preparation_overview: Salvia officinalis essential oil is commonly used to treat stress, insomnia, anxiety, pain, migraine, inflammation, respiratory issues, digestive issues, skin issues, and infection. This preparation is widely used in aromatherapy due to its common application in various settings. The effect of Salvia officinalis essential oil is often described as calming and sedative. Salvia officinalis essential oil is typically made from the leaves and flowers of the Salvia officinalis plant, which is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The process of extracting essential oil from the plant is moderately complex, requiring proper equipment and techniques to obtain a high-quality oil.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh leaves and flowers of Salvia officinalis, and clean them thoroughly with water.
Dry the leaves and flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator to prevent spoilage.
Using a steam distillation apparatus, place the dried leaves and flowers in the distillation chamber.
Heat the distillation apparatus to produce steam, which will extract the essential oil from the Salvia officinalis leaves and flowers.
Collect the extracted essential oil and filter it to remove any impurities or debris.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat minor wounds, skin irritations, and sore muscles by providing a protective barrier and reducing inflammation.
preparations.preparation_overview: Salvia officinalis salve is a herbal preparation used to treat various conditions including burns, wound healing, eczema, skin infections, insect bites, muscle spasms, digestive issues and stress relief. It's a common remedy in herbal medicine, widely used due to its effectiveness. The common effect of Salvia officinalis salve is its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in healing and reducing pain. The preparation is made from the leaves and roots of the Salvia officinalis plant, which are combined with a carrier oil to create a salve. Making Salvia officinalis salve is moderately difficult, requiring some knowledge of herbal preparation and equipment such as a double boiler and a strainer.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and roots, and dry them thoroughly in a warm, dark place.
Combine the dried leaves and roots in a mortar and pestle, and grind them into a fine powder.
Add the powdered leaves and roots to a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, and let it steep in a cool, dark place for several weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids.

Mix the infused oil with a natural wax, such as beeswax, and melt it in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, then pour the mixture into a tin or jar and let it cool and solidify.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat digestive issues, relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and manage menopausal symptoms.
preparations.preparation_overview: Salvia officinalis capsule is a herbal preparation used to treat indigestion, gastroesophageal reflux, diarrhea, stress, anxiety, insomnia, dyspepsia, and migraine. It's commonly used due to its widespread availability and proven efficacy. This capsule has a common effect of reducing symptoms of the mentioned conditions. The preparation is made from the leaves and roots of Salvia officinalis, a plant known for its medicinal properties. Its production is moderately difficult due to the need for precise harvesting and processing of the plant materials to preserve their active compounds.
preparation_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves and roots from a reputable source.
Dry the leaves and roots further to reduce moisture content, if necessary.
Grind the dried leaves and roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered leaves and roots together in a 1:1 ratio to create the herbal blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with the herbal blend and seal them to create the final product.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion and dyspepsia, and to reduce inflammation, particularly for sore throats and mouth ulcers.
preparations.preparation_overview: Salvia officinalis tea is a herbal preparation used to treat various health issues, including stomach upset, digestive issues, cough, respiratory infections, insomnia, skin irritation, wound healing, gastroesophageal reflux disease, and menstrual cramps. This preparation is extremely common, often used in traditional medicine, and is a staple in many households. Its effects are well-documented, with users consistently reporting a soothing and calming sensation after consumption. Salvia officinalis tea is typically made from the leaves of the Salvia officinalis plant, which can also be used as a root. The preparation process is straightforward, involving the steeping of the leaves or roots in hot water, making it easy to incorporate into daily routine.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried Salvia officinalis leaves and roots from a trusted source.
Rinse the leaves and roots thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the leaves and roots in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour boiling water over the leaves and roots, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, then serve the Salvia officinalis herbal tea hot or iced.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Salvia officinalis are experiencing gastrointestinal distress, which can range from mild diarrhea to severe abdominal pain, as well as showing signs of allergic reaction, such as hives and swelling, particularly if the plant is ingested in large quantities or over an extended period. In some cases, experiencing cardiovascular issues, including rapid heartbeat and blood pressure fluctuations, may occur, especially in individuals with pre-existing heart conditions. Additionally, showing liver toxicity, where liver enzymes become elevated, may be a concern, especially in individuals who consume excessive amounts of the plant or have pre-existing liver conditions. Experiencing hormonal imbalance, such as thyroid dysfunction, may also occur, particularly in individuals who consume Salvia officinalis for prolonged periods. Furthermore, experiencing neurological symptoms, including seizures and respiratory failure, is a severe and potentially life-threatening side effect, as well as kidney damage, and psychotic episodes.

precautions.precaution_name: Consult a healthcare professional before use
precautions.precaution_list_description: Due to potential interactions with medications, consult a healthcare professional before using Salvia officinalis.

precautions.precaution_name: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate
precautions.precaution_list_description: Regularly monitor blood pressure and heart rate due to Salvia officinalis's vasodilatory effects, which can lead to decreased blood pressure and bradycardia.

precautions.precaution_name: Use in moderation and for short periods
precautions.precaution_list_description: Salvia officinalis extracts should be limited to short-term consumption due to potential neuroactive effects.
Salvia officinalis contains thujone, which can stimulate uterine contractions and has abortifacient properties.

precautions.precaution_name: Do not use with sedatives or antidepressants
precautions.precaution_list_description: Combining Salvia officinalis with sedatives or antidepressants increases the risk of adverse interactions and serotonin syndrome.

precautions.precaution_name: Be aware of interactions with other medications
precautions.precaution_list_description: Salvia officinalis interacts with blood thinners, diabetes medications, and certain antidepressants.

precautions.precaution_name: Start with low doses and gradually increase
precautions.precaution_list_description: Initial doses of Salvia officinalis should not exceed 10-20 mg, and the dose can be gradually increased with caution.

precautions.precaution_name: Watch for signs of allergic reactions
precautions.precaution_list_description: Skin irritation, hives, or respiratory problems occur in individuals with salicylate or mint allergy.

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid use in children and elderly
precautions.precaution_list_description: Salvia officinalis should be used with caution in children and elderly due to decreased liver enzyme activity and increased risk of adverse effects.

precautions.precaution_name: Store properly to maintain potency and safety
precautions.precaution_list_description: Salvia officinalis requires a cool, dry, dark place to prevent degradation of essential oils and loss of medicinal properties.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", the essential oils of Salvia officinalis have been found to possess antioxidant, antidiabetic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The full flowering stage of the plant was shown to have the best inhibitory effects against α-amylase, α-glucosidase, and lipase activities, as well as a strong 5-lipoxygenase inhibitory effect. Additionally, the essential oils of Salvia officinalis demonstrated strong antibacterial activity against six bacterial strains, including bactericidal effects on certain pathogens.

According to a study published in General Dentistry, Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, effectively inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms including Staphylococcus, Streptococcus, and Candida species. The study revealed that Salvia officinalis extract showed no cytotoxic effects on murine macrophages, suggesting its safety for potential use in medical applications. Additionally, the extract was found to inhibit the production of proinflammatory cytokines, further highlighting its potential therapeutic benefits.

According to a study published by "Advances in pharmacological and pharmaceutical sciences", Salvia officinalis demonstrated remarkable antioxidant properties, showing better results in DPPH, ABTS, and FRAP tests compared to another plant. Additionally, its essential oil exhibited significant antibacterial activity against 9 out of 10 examined bacterial strains, with the exception of Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Furthermore, Salvia officinalis essential oil also showed promising antidiabetic properties, inhibiting α-amylase and α-glucosidase activities.

According to a study published by "Metabolites", the extracts of Salvia officinalis have shown potential in managing Helicobacter pylori infections due to its strong anti-H. pylori activity, comparable to the antibiotic clarithromycin. The study also found that the extracts possess anti-inflammatory properties, with the total ethanolic extract of Salvia officinalis exhibiting a significant reduction in Cox-2 expression, similar to the anti-inflammatory drug celecoxib. Additionally, the study suggests that the metabolites of Salvia officinalis, such as carnosic acid, may have a role in the management of H. pylori infections.

According to a study published by "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", the bioactive components of Salvia officinalis, particularly salvianolic acids, have shown potential as antidiabetic agents. This plant has been found to possess various biologically active components that can benefit health, including monoterpenes, diterpenes, triterpenes, and phenolic components. The study suggests that Salvia officinalis may have a positive effect on health conditions, particularly in treating diabetes.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Salvia officinalis are attributed to its ability to exhibit antimicrobial effects, thereby preventing the growth of various microorganisms. This property is largely due to the presence of volatile oils, which possess potent antimicrobial activity. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has been found to exhibit antioxidant properties, which help in scavenging free radicals and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties are also significant, as they help in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with various inflammatory disorders. Furthermore, Salvia officinalis has been reported to possess analgesic, antispasmodic, antiviral, antiseptic, cicatrizant, and hepatoprotective effects, as well as being capable of reducing pain, relaxing muscles, and protecting the liver, among other benefits.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Salvia officinalis are primarily responsible for its medicinal properties, with rosmarinic acid exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes. Ursolic acid has been shown to display anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, modulating the immune system and protecting against infections. Quercetin, a flavonoid with potent antioxidant properties, is involved in scavenging reactive oxygen species and protecting against oxidative stress. Luteolin and apigenin, other flavonoids found in Salvia officinalis, have been demonstrated to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, with luteolin also exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral effects. These constituents collectively contribute to the plant's therapeutic potential, including its use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Salvia officinalis for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are often used for their antimicrobial properties to treat wounds and skin conditions. Salvia officinalis essential oil is commonly used for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, and can be applied topically to relieve pain and reduce swelling. A salve made from the plant's leaves is also used to treat skin irritations and minor cuts, as well as to soothe sore muscles. Capsules containing Salvia officinalis extract are sometimes taken orally to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, and are also used as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Salvia officinalis tea is consumed for its expectorant properties to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Salvia officinalis medicinally are consulting a healthcare professional before use, as its effects can vary greatly from person to person, and monitoring blood pressure and heart rate due to potential cardiovascular effects. Additionally, using it in moderation and for short periods is advised to minimize side effects. Women should avoid using Salvia officinalis during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and individuals taking sedatives or antidepressants should not use it simultaneously due to potential interactions. It is also essential to be aware of interactions with other medications, start with low doses and gradually increase, watch for signs of allergic reactions, avoid use in children and the elderly, and store it properly to maintain potency and safety.
